answer: When a seed value is provided, it is used as the initial accumulator value; when it is not, the accumulator function operates over the sequence without an explicit initial value. The result selector function in the most complex overload is used to select the result value from the final accumulator.
Five distinct numeric types are supported by the `Average` method overloads: Decimal, Double, Int32, Int64, and Single. All five types have nullable variants.

SingleOrDefault throws an exception if more than one element satisfies the condition (or if there is more than one element in the sequence when no predicate is provided). This differs from FirstOrDefault and LastOrDefault, which do not throw an exception in these cases.

Use GroupBy<TSource,TKey,TElement,TResult>(IEnumerable<TSource>, Func<TSource,TKey>, Func<TSource,TElement>, Func<TKey,IEnumerable<TElement>,TResult>, IEqualityComparer<TKey>) or GroupBy<TSource,TKey,TElement,TResult>(IEnumerable<TSource>, Func<TSource,TKey>, Func<TSource,TElement>, Func<TKey,IEnumerable<TElement>,TResult>) to group elements by a key, project each group's elements using a specified function, and create a result value from each group and its key.
